message:
RPC bench server attaches to vite preview too — preview needs no extra steps

Vite plugins have a configurePreviewServer hook that's the preview-mode
counterpart to configureServer. Refactored the upgrade-handler logic out
into attachRpc(httpServer, …) and called it from both hooks so dev and
preview get the same /capnwasm + /capnweb endpoints alongside their
static-file handling.

Verified: vite preview at :4173 now hosts both endpoints; the RPC
bench page runs end-to-end against the production build with no
second process. capnwasm wins 3 of 3 in this run:

  burst    14.5 µs/call vs 65.5 µs/call   4.52× faster
  pipeline 200 µs       vs 300 µs         1.50× faster
  blob     800 µs       vs 1.40 ms        1.75× faster (128 KB vs 171 KB wire)

The standalone `npm run server` still exists for people hosting the
static dist/ behind a CDN where they have to run the WS handlers as a
separate process. Updated the docs + the rpc.html "server unreachable"
banner to point at that path explicitly instead of saying "preview
won't work".

files.patch:
@@ -30,8 +30,15 @@ npm run build        # static output in web/dist
 npm run preview      # serve the built site at http://localhost:4173
 ```
 
-The build emits hashed asset filenames for the wasm + JS, so it’s safe
-to deploy behind any CDN with long-cache headers.
+`npm run preview` also attaches the RPC bench server to its HTTP port,
+so `/rpc.html` works against the production build out of the box. The
+build emits hashed asset filenames for the wasm + JS, so the static
+`dist/` is safe to drop behind any CDN with long-cache headers.
+
+If you do drop the static `dist/` behind a CDN, the RPC bench page
+will need a real WebSocket backend somewhere — `npm run server` runs
+the same handlers as a standalone process at `ws://HOST:8081`. Or wire
+the handlers in `vite-rpc-server.mjs` into your own deployment.
 
 ## Wiring
